symbols.name: \(\mathcal R^{\mathrm{rw}}\)
symbols.space: \(\mathcal L^2\times\mathcal M^{2|\mathcal L|}\times\mathcal Y^{2|\mathcal L||\mathcal M|}\)

symbols.name: \(m_a(r)\)
symbols.def: Mediator response indexed by treatment and simple-mediation response type.

symbols.name: \(y_{am}(r)\)
symbols.def: Outcome response indexed by treatment, mediator, and simple-mediation response type.

symbols.name: \(\ell_a(r^{\mathrm{rw}})\)
symbols.def: Recanting-witness response indexed by treatment and response type.

symbols.name: \(m_{a\ell}(r^{\mathrm{rw}})\)
symbols.def: Mediator response indexed by treatment, witness, and response type.

symbols.name: \(y_{a\ell m}(r^{\mathrm{rw}})\)
symbols.def: Outcome response indexed by treatment, witness, mediator, and response type.

symbols.name: \(M(a,\ell)\)
symbols.def: Mediator potential outcome in the recanting-witness graph.

symbols.name: \(Y(a,\ell,m)\)
symbols.def: Outcome potential outcome in the recanting-witness graph.

symbols.name: \(\sigma_2(B)\)
symbols.def: Second singular value of a matrix \(B\).

symbols.name: \(\mathfrak M_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\)
symbols.def: Observed-data fiber of the simple-mediation SCM class.

symbols.name: \(\mathfrak M_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{rw}}(P^{\mathrm{rw}})\)
symbols.def: Observed-data fiber of the recanting-witness SCM class.

novel.justification: The graph combines randomized treatment, an unmeasured mediator--outcome risk state, and two pre-treatment negative-control measurements, a structure used in mediated clinical and social-science studies.
A fixed finite sensitivity analysis treats each of three substantively specified risk strata as present instead of allowing a redundant latent label.

assumptions.condition: \(\forall(z,w,r,u)\in\mathcal Z\times\mathcal W\times\mathcal R\times\mathcal U:P^\star(Z=z,W=w,R=r\mid U=u)=q^Z_{zu}q^W_{wu}P^\star(R=r\mid U=u)\)

novel.justification: Independent administrative and laboratory measurement channels can share latent risk while retaining conditionally independent measurement errors.
A treatment-side administrative proxy can record a common coarse risk tier while two latent states retain unrecorded within-tier severity.
An outcome-side laboratory proxy can record the same coarse risk tier while two latent states retain unrecorded within-tier severity.

The proxies retain two quantitatively stable directions even though shared coarsening removes the third; the explicit seed in the feasible-domain definition shows this condition is nonvacuous throughout the declared parameter range.
The extension records an observed treatment-induced mediator--outcome confounder whose shared response values create the recanting-witness cross-world constraint.
The delegated finite extension retains the same active three-state risk resolution as the primary SCM.
The extension preserves the two-channel negative-control measurement model while changing the causal response type.
The treatment-proxy bridge route is explicitly rank deficient at every conditional stratum in the delegated extension.
The outcome-proxy bridge route is explicitly rank deficient at every conditional stratum in the delegated extension.
The joint-proxy bridge route is explicitly rank deficient at every conditional stratum in the delegated extension.

definitions.name: \(\mathcal G\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal G:\ U\to R,\ U\to Z,\ U\to W,\ A\to M,\ A\to Y,\ R\to M,\ R\to Y,\ M\to Y\).

definitions.name: \(\mathcal G^{\mathrm{rw}}\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal G^{\mathrm{rw}}:\ U\to R^{\mathrm{rw}},\ U\to Z,\ U\to W,\ A\to L,\ A\to M,\ A\to Y,\ R^{\mathrm{rw}}\to L,\ R^{\mathrm{rw}}\to M,\ R^{\mathrm{rw}}\to Y,\ L\to M,\ L\to Y,\ M\to Y\).

definitions.name: \(\mathcal R,\mathcal R^{\mathrm{rw}}\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal R=\{((m_a)_{a\in\{0,1\}},(y_{am})_{a\in\{0,1\},m\in\mathcal M}):m_a\in\mathcal M,y_{am}\in\mathcal Y\}\) and \(\mathcal R^{\mathrm{rw}}=\{((\ell_a)_{a\in\{0,1\}},(m_{a\ell})_{a\in\{0,1\},\ell\in\mathcal L},(y_{a\ell m})_{a\in\{0,1\},\ell\in\mathcal L,m\in\mathcal M}):\ell_a\in\mathcal L,m_{a\ell}\in\mathcal M,y_{a\ell m}\in\mathcal Y\}\).

definitions.name: simple-mediation proxy operators
definitions.construction: \([\mathsf C^Z_{am}g](z)=\sum_{u\in\mathcal U}g(u)q^Z_{zu}\sum_{r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}\mathbf 1\{m=m_a(r)\}\), \([\mathsf C^W_{am}g](w)=\sum_{u\in\mathcal U}g(u)q^W_{wu}\sum_{r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}\mathbf 1\{m=m_a(r)\}\), and \([\mathsf C^{ZW}_{am}g](z,w)=\sum_{u\in\mathcal U}g(u)q^Z_{zu}q^W_{wu}\sum_{r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}\mathbf 1\{m=m_a(r)\}\).

definitions.name: recanting-witness proxy operators
definitions.construction: For \(V\in\{Z,W,ZW\}\), define \(\mathsf C^{V,\mathrm{rw}}_{a\ell m}\) by the corresponding formula in \(def:conditional-operators\), replacing \(\lambda_{ur}\mathbf 1\{m=m_a(r)\}\) with \(\lambda^{\mathrm{rw}}_{ur}\mathbf 1\{\ell=\ell_a(r),m=m_{a\ell}(r)\}\); use \(q^Z_{zu}\), \(q^W_{wu}\), or their product for \(V=Z,W,ZW\), respectively.

definitions.name: \(P^{\mathrm{seed}}\)
definitions.construction: Set \(P(A=a)=1/2\), \(\lambda_{ur}=1/(3|\mathcal R|)\), and \(q^Z_{0,1}=q^Z_{0,2}=q^W_{0,1}=q^W_{0,2}=3/4\), \(q^Z_{0,3}=q^W_{0,3}=1/4\), with complementary probability for value \(1\); let \(P^{\mathrm{seed}}\) be the observed law induced by these primitives.

definitions.name: \(\mathscr H(P)\)
definitions.construction: For rational \(P\), encode \(\mathcal F_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\) by polynomial equalities and inequalities, replace singular-value restrictions by principal-minor inequalities, append \(\psi(\lambda)=L_0(P)\) or \(\psi(\lambda)=U_0(P)\), and seek a Positivstellensatz infeasibility certificate for the appended face.

definitions.name: recanting-witness feasible sets
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal F_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{rw}}(P^{\mathrm{rw}})\) is the set defined by \(def:proxy-feasible-set\) after replacing \(r,\lambda_{ur},m_a(r),y_{a,m_a(r)}(r),p_{zwmy\mid a}\) by \(r^{\mathrm{rw}},\lambda^{\mathrm{rw}}_{ur},(\ell_a(r^{\mathrm{rw}}),m_{a\ell_a(r^{\mathrm{rw}})}(r^{\mathrm{rw}})),y_{a,\ell_a(r^{\mathrm{rw}}),m_{a\ell_a(r^{\mathrm{rw}})}(r^{\mathrm{rw}})}(r^{\mathrm{rw}}),p^{\mathrm{rw}}_{zw\ell my\mid a}\), and by imposing \(ass:rw-incomplete-z\), \(ass:rw-incomplete-w\), and \(ass:rw-incomplete-zw\); \(\mathcal F_0^{\mathrm{rw}}(P^{\mathrm{rw}})\) is its response-type-margin version after summing over \(z,w\) and deleting proxy restrictions.

definitions.name: \(\mathscr H_{\mathrm{rw}}(P^{\mathrm{rw}})\)
definitions.construction: Encode \(\mathcal F_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{rw}}(P^{\mathrm{rw}})\), append equality of \(\psi^{\mathrm{rw}}\) to either endpoint of \(\Theta_0^{\mathrm{rw}}(P^{\mathrm{rw}})\), and seek a Positivstellensatz infeasibility certificate for that endpoint face.

statements.statement: For every \(\kappa\in(0,1/3]\) and \(\eta\in(0,1/48]\), \(P^{\mathrm{seed}}\in\mathcal D_{\kappa,\eta}^+\); in particular, each conditional operator in \(ass:conditional-separation\) has second singular value strictly exceeding \(1/48\) at the seed.
statements.justification: The declared index range must describe a nonempty model before an observed-law witness is sought.
statements.gap: Existing completeness discussions identify cardinality failure but do not provide this explicit positive-cell failed-completeness interior calibration.
statements.consumer: It licenses the strict-contraction question on a nonvacuous parameter region.

statements.statement: For every \(P^\star\in\mathfrak M_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}\) and every \((a,m)\in\{0,1\}\times\mathcal M\), \(\operatorname{rank}(\mathsf C^Z_{am})=\operatorname{rank}(\mathsf C^W_{am})=\operatorname{rank}(\mathsf C^{ZW}_{am})=2<3\).
statements.justification: The common tier makes states one and two indistinguishable, while separation preserves two nonzero directions.
statements.gap: Existing proximal mediation work assumes the requisite bridge completeness; this specifies a failure regime common to every simple-mediation conditional proxy view.
statements.consumer: It verifies that the headline operates outside the available bridge-injectivity routes.

statements.statement: For every \(P\in\mathcal D_{\kappa,\eta}^+\), \(\{E_{P^\star}[Y(1,M(0))-Y(0,M(0))]:P^\star\in\mathfrak M_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\}=\Theta_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\), with endpoints attained in the compatible-SCM fiber.
statements.justification: This supporting specialization fixes the exact graph-compatible SCM fiber over which endpoint exclusion is posed.
statements.gap: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 supplies general discrete-SCM optimization; this is its graph-compatible mediation specialization.
statements.consumer: It makes the target interval an observed-data-computable functional for the declared SCM class.

statements.statement: For every \(P\in\mathcal D_{\kappa,\eta}^+\), \(\Theta_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\subseteq\Theta_0(P)\), so \(L_0(P)\le L_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\le U_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\le U_0(P)\).
statements.justification: Marginalizing a graph-compatible proxy completion leaves a valid response-type completion of the no-proxy mediation margins.
statements.gap: Sjölander2009 and BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 provide the no-proxy floor; containment is supporting structure for its strict refinement.
statements.consumer: It establishes the comparison baseline for endpoint exclusion.

statements.statement: When \(Z\) and \(W\) are singleton variables and proxy restrictions are deleted, the projection of \(\mathcal F_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\) onto response-type masses equals \(\mathcal F_0(P)\).
statements.justification: The SCM program has the standard no-information reduction.
statements.gap: This is a reduction check, not a novelty claim.
statements.consumer: It links the proposed interval to the binary randomized-treatment no-proxy endpoint formulas.

statements.statement: Construct rational \(P^\dagger\in\mathcal D_{\kappa,\eta}^+\), \(\epsilon>0\), and an endpoint-face exclusion certificate in \(\mathscr H(P^\dagger)\) such that \(\mathcal N_\epsilon(P^\dagger)\subseteq\mathcal D_{\kappa,\eta}^+\) and either \(L_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)>L_0(P)\) for every \(P\in\mathcal N_\epsilon(P^\dagger)\) or \(U_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)<U_0(P)\) for every \(P\in\mathcal N_\epsilon(P^\dagger)\).
statements.justification: A positive-cell certificate would show that incomplete but stable proxy measurements have observable content beyond the no-proxy mediation floor.
statements.gap: BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 and Sjölander2009 give no-proxy mediation bounds, whereas GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 gives no-completeness proxy bounds including hidden-mediator cross-world contrasts. Neither states this observed-mediator, two-channel factorized strict-contraction certificate.
statements.consumer: It supplies a checkable finite sensitivity regime in which negative-control proxies shorten the established natural-direct-effect interval.

statements.statement: For the declared graph \(\mathcal G^{\mathrm{rw}}\), construct a positive-cell observed law \(P^{\mathrm{rw}}\) and a certificate in \(\mathscr H_{\mathrm{rw}}(P^{\mathrm{rw}})\) that strictly excludes an endpoint of \(\Theta_0^{\mathrm{rw}}(P^{\mathrm{rw}})\) from \(\Theta_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{rw}}(P^{\mathrm{rw}})\) after the three conditional proxy routes declared in \(ass:rw-incomplete-z\), \(ass:rw-incomplete-w\), and \(ass:rw-incomplete-zw\) fail completeness.
statements.justification: The complete extension preserves the primary simple-mediation kernel while posing a precisely typed recanting-witness transfer.
statements.gap: BaiWuSunCui2026 and WuBaiCui2026 identify recanting-witness effects through proximal bridges under completeness, not a factorization-constrained set after each declared conditional operator is rank deficient.
statements.consumer: A resolution exports the certified simple-mediation architecture to a graph with an observed recanting witness.

sampling_model.units: Each unit follows a graph-compatible finite NPSEM with latent \(U\) and \(R\).
sampling_model.treatment: \(A\) is randomized with positive assignment probabilities.
sampling_model.overlap: Every treatment-conditional observed cell is positive on \(\mathcal D_{\kappa,\eta}^+\).
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: This is a population identified-set proposal; estimation and inference are outside scope.

tldr: The primary object is the graph-native simple-mediation natural direct effect. A finite SCM with an active three-state latent risk variable, two factorized negative-control proxies, and shared two-tier measurement coarsening makes every simple-mediation conditional bridge operator noninjective while retaining two stable measurement directions. The exact compatible-SCM response-function program is supporting machinery. The field-level question is a rational positive-cell observed law with a semialgebraically certified open neighborhood on which the proxy interval strictly contracts the Sjölander--Breum no-proxy floor. The parameter range is nonvacuous by an explicit rational interior seed. A fully specified recanting-witness path-specific effect is delegated as a downstream open-ended extension.

project_justification.gap: Completeness-based proximal mediation point identifies natural effects only under injective conditional proxy operators, whereas no-proxy mediation bounds discard proxy factorization. No verified work supplies an observed-data endpoint-face exclusion certificate in this graph-compatible failed-completeness regime.
project_justification.niche: The SCM preserves the nested counterfactual coupling in \(Y(1,M(0))\), fixes an active three-state risk resolution, and derives failed completeness from a substantive shared measurement tier rather than an accidental rank equality.
project_justification.fill: The project defines a nonempty compatible-SCM fiber, proves its exact response-function representation, and seeks a rational positive-cell strict contraction that persists on an observed-data neighborhood.

related_work: Sjölander2009 gives foundational randomized-treatment bounds for natural direct effects with a confounded intermediate variable. BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 derives sharp no-proxy natural and separable mediation bounds; Open-ended Question 1 is explicitly a strict tightening of that floor in the declared finite SCM. DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 point identifies natural effects with proximal bridges under completeness, whereas shared-tier measurement here forces failed completeness. GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 supplies no-completeness proxy bounds, including cross-world hidden-mediator contrasts, but does not encode the observed-mediator response-type coupling and two-channel factorization here. DorettiStanghellini2024 imposes partially constant cross-world dependence without finite proxy factorization, and ZhangSu2024 assumes partial proxy-transition knowledge; both are non-nested informed-by models. BaiWuSunCui2026 and WuBaiCui2026 motivate the delegated recanting-witness extension. DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 supplies the discrete SCM optimization machinery for the supporting representation.
technical_internal_limitation: The supporting finite response-function program is nonconvex polynomial optimization. The proposal makes no computational-complexity claim; the certificate handle is retained only to establish a selected endpoint-face exclusion.
honest_scope: All claims are conditional on the stated graph-compatible three-state positive-mass SCM, proxy factorization, shared coarsening, and separation conditions. The core does not claim unrestricted-cardinality sharpness, point identification, inference, or reduction to the hidden-mediator proxy bounds of GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023. The strict-contraction and recanting-witness certificates remain open. With singleton proxies and deleted proxy restrictions, the simple-mediation program reduces to the standard no-proxy response-function set.

bibliography.citation: Arvid Sjölander (2009), Bounds on Natural Direct Effects in the Presence of Confounded Intermediate Variables, \emph{Statistics in Medicine} 28, 558--571.
Marie S. Breum, Vanessa Didelez, Erin E. Gabriel, and Michael C. Sachs (2025), Bounds for Causal Mediation Effects, \emph{arXiv:2512.11549}.
Marco Doretti and Elena Stanghellini (2024), Interval Identification of Natural Effects in the Presence of Outcome-Related Unmeasured Confounding, \emph{arXiv:2405.13621}.

Zhiheng Zhang and Xinyan Su (2024), Partial Identification with Proxy of Latent Confoundings via Sum-of-ratios Fractional Programming, \emph{Proceedings of the Fortieth Conference on Uncertainty in Artificial Intelligence}, PMLR 244, 4140--4172.
Oliver Dukes, Ilya Shpitser, and Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023), Proximal Mediation Analysis, \emph{Biometrika} 110, 973--987.
AmirEmad Ghassami, Ilya Shpitser, and Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023), Partial Identification of Causal Effects Using Proxy Variables, \emph{arXiv:2304.04374}.
Yang Bai, Sihan Wu, Baoluo Sun, and Yifan Cui (2026), Proximal Path-Specific Inference, \emph{arXiv:2605.09462}.
Sihan Wu, Yang Bai, and Yifan Cui (2026), Proximal Mediation Analysis with Hidden Recanting Witnesses, \emph{arXiv:2606.17600}.

Guilherme Duarte, Noam Finkelstein, Dean Knox, Jonathan Mummolo, and Ilya Shpitser (2024), An Automated Approach to Causal Inference in Discrete Settings, \emph{Journal of the American Statistical Association} 119, 1778--1793.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Bounds are derived for natural direct effects with randomized treatment and a confounded intermediate variable.
Sharp no-proxy bounds are derived for natural and separable mediation effects under unmeasured intermediate-variable confounding.
Binary natural-effect intervals are derived under partially constant cross-world dependence.
Proxy bounds are computed without completeness under partial knowledge of the proxy transition matrix.
Proximal bridge strategies point identify natural direct and indirect effects under completeness.
No-completeness proxy bounds are derived for treatment effects and hidden-mediator cross-world contrasts.
Bridge-based strategies identify recanting-witness path-specific effects under completeness.
Proximal strategies identify path-specific effects with hidden recanting witnesses.
Finite discrete SCM queries admit constrained response-function optimization representations.

novelty_justification.repo_axis: The focal object is a robust positive-cell strict contraction in a graph-native compatible-SCM fiber; the response-function program is supporting machinery and the recanting target is delegated.
novelty_justification.published_axis: The strict endpoint-exclusion target tightens the no-proxy natural-effect floor of Sjölander2009 and BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 under a failed-completeness proxy factorization. GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 includes hidden-mediator cross-world proxy bounds but not this observed-mediator response-type coupling.

literature_checklist.author: Sjölander
literature_checklist.one_line: Foundational no-proxy natural-direct-effect floor targeted by strict contraction.

literature_checklist.author: Breum et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Sharp no-proxy natural and separable mediation bounds are the immediate comparator.

literature_checklist.author: Dukes, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
literature_checklist.one_line: Completeness-based proximal point identification defines the boundary regime.

literature_checklist.author: Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
literature_checklist.one_line: No-completeness proxy bounds including hidden-mediator cross-world contrasts motivate the observed-mediator distinction.

literature_checklist.author: Bai et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Recanting-witness proximal identification motivates the fully specified delegated extension.

literature_checklist.author: Duarte et al.
literature_checklist.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
literature_checklist.one_line: Discrete SCM optimization supplies the supporting exact-program method.
